name: Qwen3.5
description: Alibaba's Apache-2.0 flagship open model: Qwen3.5-397B-A17B, a 512-expert hybrid MoE (397B total / 17B active), natively multimodal, 262K context (1M on hosted Qwen3.5-Plus), 201 languages; beats Alibaba's API-only 1T Qwen3-Max with up to 19x faster long-context decode. Still its largest open-weight model as of July 2026, but smaller Apache-2.0 Qwen3.6 models (Apr 2026) surpass it on agentic coding, and the newest frontier (Qwen3.7-Max, May 2026; Qwen3.7-Plus, Jun 2026) is API-only.

evidence.value: Strong agentic coding results; flagship 397B-A17B surpasses Qwen3-Max on coding benchmarks
Independent reporting confirms 397B-A17B beats the 1T-parameter, API-only Qwen3-Max

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Vendor benchmarks corroborated by independent press coverage and community leaderboards

evidence.value: Frontier-class math and agentic reasoning under the 'Towards Native Multimodal Agents' positioning
Detailed benchmark tables across reasoning suites on the official model card

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Mathematical and agentic reasoning benchmarks from the model card and release blog, cross-checked against community evaluations

evidence.value: Natively multimodal (text + vision) with strong general knowledge across 201 languages

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Comprehensive knowledge and multimodal benchmark review including multilingual coverage

evidence.value: Stable hybrid-MoE routing; consistent outputs across repeated runs in community testing

output_consistency.methodology: Repeated-prompt testing across temperature settings, supplemented by community reports

latency_p50.value: 1.5s (hosted); 17B active params enable fast self-hosted decode

evidence.value: Up to 19x faster decode than Qwen3-Max at 256K context thanks to the sparse 17B-active design

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency on hosted endpoints and decode-throughput comparisons from independent reporting

evidence.value: p95 ~3.8s on hosted endpoints for standard workloads

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads from independent benchmarking

evidence.value: 262K native context for open weights; hosted Qwen3.5-Plus extends to 1M tokens

context_window.methodology: Official specification from model card and Alibaba Cloud documentation

evidence.value: Stable availability on Alibaba Cloud (incl. Singapore region) plus many third-party hosts and self-hosting

uptime.methodology: Hosted-platform availability history plus redundancy across third-party hosts

performance_reliability.notes: Beats Alibaba's own 1T-parameter API-only Qwen3-Max with only 17B active parameters, with up to 19x faster decode at 256K context. Native multimodality and 201-language coverage are unmatched among open models.

evidence.value: Good resistance to common injection patterns; multimodal inputs add an image-based injection surface

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Testing against OWASP LLM01 prompt injection patterns, including image-borne injection for multimodal inputs

evidence.value: Safety post-training across the family; open weights mean alignment is removable downstream

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Adversarial prompt testing; assessment accounts for open-weight modifiability

evidence.value: Standard data handling on hosted endpoints; self-hosting gives complete data control

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of hosted-platform policies plus the self-hosting option for full data isolation

evidence.value: Multilingual safety filtering across 201 languages; refusal behavior consistent in community testing

output_safety.methodology: Safety testing across harmful content categories and multiple languages on default weights

evidence.value: API key authentication, HTTPS, RAM-based access control, and rate limiting on Alibaba Cloud

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features on the first-party hosted platform

security.notes: Solid default guardrails with notably broad multilingual safety coverage. Multimodal inputs widen the attack surface; open weights shift responsibility to deployers who fine-tune.

data_residency.value: China/Singapore (Alibaba Cloud first-party); anywhere via self-hosting or third-party hosts

evidence.value: First-party hosting on Alibaba Cloud is China-jurisdiction (with a Singapore international region); Apache-2.0 weights allow deployment in any jurisdiction

data_residency.methodology: Review of hosting regions and licensing; China-jurisdiction caveat applies to Alibaba's first-party API, not self-hosted or Western-hosted deployments

evidence.value: Enterprise tier does not train on customer data; self-hosting removes the concern entirely

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of hosted-platform data usage terms

data_retention.value: Per Alibaba Cloud policy (region-dependent); zero when self-hosted

evidence.value: Hosted retention follows Alibaba Cloud regional policies; self-hosted deployments retain nothing externally

data_retention.methodology: Review of hosted-platform retention policies; retention is deployment-dependent for open-weight models

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction; Alibaba Cloud provides surrounding data-governance tooling

pii_handling.methodology: Review of data protection capabilities and customer responsibilities

evidence.value: Alibaba Cloud holds ISO 27001/SOC reports for its infrastructure, but no HIPAA/FedRAMP path for the model service; Western-host deployments inherit those hosts' certifications

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of infrastructure certifications versus model-service-level compliance for Western regulated markets

evidence.value: No zero-retention guarantee on first-party hosting; self-hosting provides true zero external retention

zero_data_retention.methodology: Review of data handling across first-party API, third-party hosts, and self-hosting

privacy_compliance.notes: Alibaba's first-party API is China-jurisdiction (Singapore region available), which concerns Western regulated buyers; Apache-2.0 self-hosting or Western third-party hosting fully avoids that. Alibaba Cloud's infrastructure certifications are stronger than DeepSeek's platform but still lack HIPAA/FedRAMP for the model service.

evidence.value: Hybrid thinking modes expose reasoning traces; fully inspectable when self-hosted

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and trace accessibility

evidence.value: Moderate hallucination rate, improved over Qwen3; grounding quality on vision inputs is strong

hallucination_rate.methodology: Testing on factual QA and multimodal grounding datasets

evidence.value: Broad multilingual fairness work; topic-avoidance on China-politically-sensitive subjects persists in default weights

bias_fairness.methodology: Evaluation on bias benchmarks across languages and politically sensitive topic probes

evidence.value: Expresses uncertainty in thinking mode; final-answer calibration is adequate but not exceptional

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.value: Thorough model card with architecture details (512-expert hybrid MoE), benchmarks, usage guidance, and deployment recipes

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of model card and technical documentation completeness

evidence.value: Training methodology and multilingual/multimodal data strategy described at a high level; detailed composition not disclosed

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Multilingual safety alignment in released weights; removable by downstream fine-tuning

guardrails.methodology: Analysis of built-in safety mechanisms in default weights

trust_transparency.notes: Strong open documentation and inspectable reasoning. Typical open-model gaps remain: limited training-data detail and topic-avoidance on politically sensitive subjects in default weights.

evidence.value: OpenAI-compatible API with function calling, multimodal inputs, and hybrid thinking-mode controls

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: Day-one support in vLLM, SGLang, and transformers; actively maintained official repos

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK and inference-framework support

evidence.value: Fast release cadence (supersedes Qwen3 family and Qwen2.5-VL); open weights remain permanently available, softening deprecation impact
Qwen3.6 open models shipped Apr 2026 (35B-A3B on 04-16, 27B dense on 04-22, Apache 2.0, 256K context): the 27B dense outperforms the 397B-A17B Qwen3.5 flagship on agentic coding benchmarks

Family frontier moved to proprietary API-only models: Qwen3.7-Max (2026-05-20, Apsara Summit) and Qwen3.7-Plus (2026-06-01), 1M context, agentic positioning; Alibaba keeps its newest flagship closed-weight

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of release cadence and weight-availability guarantees

evidence.value: Usage dashboards and logging on Alibaba Cloud; full observability when self-hosting

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of monitoring tools across deployment options

evidence.value: Alibaba Cloud offers paid enterprise support tiers; Western-market support depth lags US hyperscalers; strong community channels

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of support tiers, documentation, and community responsiveness

evidence.value: Largest open-model ecosystem by derivative count; full size ladder from 397B-A17B and 122B-A10B down to 0.8B released Feb-Mar 2026

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of derivative models, third-party hosting, and tooling integrations

evidence.value: Apache 2.0 across the family: unrestricted commercial use with explicit patent grant

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Best-in-class open-model ecosystem: Apache 2.0 with patent grant, day-one inference-framework support, and a complete size ladder (0.8B to 397B-A17B) for matching capability to hardware. Supersedes the Qwen3 family and Qwen2.5-VL. Since April-June 2026 the family has continued with the open Qwen3.6 models (Apache 2.0) and the proprietary API-only Qwen3.7-Max/Plus.

code-generation.notes: Strong agentic coding that beats the 1T-parameter Qwen3-Max; 17B active params make self-hosted coding assistants economical.

customer-support.notes: 201-language coverage and fast decode make it a standout for global multilingual support; smaller variants serve high-volume tiers cheaply.

content-creation.notes: Strong multilingual content with native image understanding for visually grounded writing.

data-analysis.notes: Native multimodality handles charts, tables, and documents directly; 262K context (1M on Plus) covers large datasets.

research-assistant.notes: Multimodal document understanding plus long context suits literature and mixed-media research; 19x decode speedup keeps long-context work responsive.

legal-compliance.notes: First-party hosting is China-jurisdiction; viable for regulated legal work only via self-hosting or certified Western hosts.

healthcare.notes: No HIPAA path on first-party hosting; self-hosted deployment in compliant infrastructure is the only viable route.

financial-analysis.notes: Good quantitative reasoning with native chart/table understanding; data-residency planning required for regulated workloads.

education.notes: 201 languages, multimodal input, and a size ladder down to 0.8B make it exceptional for global and on-device education deployments.

creative-writing.notes: Capable multilingual creative output with visual grounding; prose distinctiveness behind dedicated creative leaders.

strengths: Beats Alibaba's own 1T-parameter API-only Qwen3-Max with just 17B active parameters (397B total)
Natively multimodal open model: text + vision under 'Towards Native Multimodal Agents'
Up to 19x faster decode than Qwen3-Max at 256K context; 262K native context (1M on hosted Qwen3.5-Plus)
201-language coverage, the broadest of any open model
Apache 2.0 license with patent grant across the entire family
Complete size ladder (0.8B to 397B-A17B, Feb-Mar 2026) for matching capability to hardware
Day-one vLLM/SGLang/transformers support and the largest open-model derivative ecosystem
limitations: First-party Alibaba Cloud hosting is China-jurisdiction (Singapore region available); no HIPAA/FedRAMP path for the model service — self-hosting or Western hosts avoid this
No longer the family's coding leader: the much smaller Apache-2.0 Qwen3.6-27B (Apr 2026) outperforms it on agentic coding benchmarks, and the newest family frontier (Qwen3.7-Max/Plus) is API-only
1M context requires the hosted Qwen3.5-Plus; open weights cap at 262K
Topic-avoidance on politically sensitive subjects in default weights
Training-data composition disclosed only at a high level
397B total parameters still require multi-GPU infrastructure to self-host despite the sparse 17B-active design
Western-market enterprise support depth lags US hyperscalers
best_for: Global multilingual products needing broad language coverage (201 languages)
Multimodal agents processing documents, charts, and images natively under an open license
Cost-efficient self-hosted inference exploiting the 17B-active sparse design
Organizations standardizing on one model family across sizes from edge (0.8B) to flagship
Long-context workloads needing fast decode at 256K tokens
not_recommended_for: Regulated Western workloads via Alibaba's first-party API
Teams needing 1M context with open weights (hosted-only feature)
Organizations without GPU infrastructure that also cannot accept China-jurisdiction hosted APIs

pricing.input: Free weights (Apache 2.0); hosted from ~$0.40 per 1M tokens on Alibaba Cloud Model Studio
pricing.output: Hosted from ~$1.20 per 1M tokens; third-party hosts vary
pricing.notes: Self-hosting is infrastructure-cost-only; the 17B-active design keeps serving costs low for its capability class. Hosted Qwen3.5-Plus (1M context) priced separately.

metadata.architecture: Hybrid Mixture-of-Experts with 512 experts (397B total / 17B active), natively multimodal, hybrid thinking modes
